case_title: KYLE KATZ VS. PIZZA FOODS, INC. A CORPORATION ET AL

docket_entries.description: JURY FEES DEPOSITED BY PLAINTIFF KATZ, KYLE AN INDIVIDUAL
REPLY OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT S MOTION TO STRIKE PRAYERS FOR RELIEF FROM PLAINTIFF S COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 59767472) FILED BY DEFENDANT PIZZA FOODS, INC.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT PIZZA FOODS, INC.'S DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE PER SE AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR UNSAFE CONDITIONS. WHILE NEGLIGENCE PER SE IS NOT A SEPARATE CLAIM FROM NEGLIGENCE, IT IS USEFUL TO HAVE EACH NEGLIGENCE THEORY LAID OUT IN A SEPARATE CAUSE OF ACTION. THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS ENTIRELY DUPLICATIVE AND ADDS NOTHING BY WAY OF FACTS OR LEGAL THEORY TO THE PRECEDING FOUR CAUSES OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT PIZZA FOODS, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PRAYERS FOR RELIEF FROM PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PIZZA FOODS, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND ATTORNEY'S FEES ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN ITS ENTIRETY WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF KYLE KATZ DOES NOT ALLEGE ANY FACTS SUGGESTING THAT HE HAS A VIABLE BASIS FOR OBTAINING EITHER PUNITIVE DAMAGES OR ATTORNEY'S FEES IN THIS CASE. IF MR. KATZ OBTAINS SUCH FACTS DURING DISCOVERY OR HIS FACTUAL INVESTIGATION, HE MAY FILE A MOTION SEEKING LEAVE TO ADD REQUESTS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND/OR ATTORNEY'S FEES. THE POSSIBILITY THAT MR. KATZ MAY BE ENTITLED TO COST OF PROOF SANCTIONS IS NOT A BASIS FOR SEEKING FEES IN A PLEADING. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-09-2018 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-31-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Tamarah P. Prevost of the law offices of Cotchett Pitre & McCarthy (650-697-6000) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

Stipulation and order appointing judge pro tem is signed and filed.

Plaintiff counsel reports that this action has settled in principle, pending notice of settlement, to be filed. Plaintiff counsel withdraws the substantive part of the motion and indicated the parties have reached an agreement as to sanctions.

The Court vacates the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF KYLE KATZ's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions is OFF CALENDAR as WITHDRAWN as to production of documents. The parties shall submit a Stipulation and [proposed] written order regarding sanctions which shall be made payable one week from this date. The parties shall file a notice of settlement upon payment of sanctions. The stipulation and [proposed] order may be submitted directly to Judge Pro Tem Steven B. Stein, via email to: sbs@sbslawsf.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven B., Stein: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (DISC302)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-02-2017 Continued To Jan-29-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-22-2017 Set For Trial On Oct-10-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-16-2017 Continued To Apr-05-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-30-2016 Set For Order To Show Cause On Mar-07-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-09-2016 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Pizza Foods, Inc.'s demurrer is overruled as to the third and fourth causes of action for negligence per se and sustained without leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action for unsafe conditions. While negligence per se is not a separate claim from negligence, it is useful to have each negligence theory laid out in a separate cause of action. The fifth cause of action is entirely duplicative and adds nothing by way of facts or legal theory to the preceding four causes of action. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Motion To Strike Prayers For Relief From Plaintiff Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Nov-09-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Pizza Foods, Inc.'s motion to strike the punitive damages and attorney's fees allegations in the complaint is granted in its entirety without leave to amend. Plaintiff Kyle Katz does not allege any facts suggesting that he has a viable basis for obtaining either punitive damages or attorney's fees in this case. If Mr. Katz obtains such facts during discovery or his factual investigation, he may file a motion seeking leave to add requests for punitive damages and/or attorney's fees. The possibility that Mr. Katz may be entitled to cost of proof sanctions is not a basis for seeking fees in a pleading. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
